steps.title: Spot the setup: problem and supporting detail
steps.content: The underlined portion identifies a characteristic that sounds like a flaw—the book's “abrupt ending”—but immediately provides a key detail: the next book “picks up neatly where Master and Commander leaves off.” This combination signals what the text is trying to accomplish.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice C describes the function as offering a detail that explains why a potential flaw isn't necessarily one.

This is exactly what happens: the seamless continuation with the next book provides grounds for arguing the ending isn't actually problematic.

The other choices misdirect by focusing on reader preferences (A, D) or attributing a later comparison to this section (B).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
